chunk_summary: This section focuses on antenatal lactation risk assessment and counseling, emphasizing evidence-based strategies to promote breastfeeding during the prenatal period, supported by global and national guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of antenatal lactation risk assessment in promoting breastfeeding success.
Identify key counseling strategies for expectant mothers to address breastfeeding challenges.
Familiarize with global and national guidelines for breastfeeding promotion during the prenatal period.
Learn to implement evidence-based practices for supporting lactation in maternity care settings.
key_facts: Clinical Protocol #19 (2024) provides updated guidelines for breastfeeding promotion during the prenatal period.
The WHO recommends integrating breastfeeding counseling into routine antenatal care to improve breastfeeding initiation rates.
The MAA (Mother’s Absolute Affection) program offers a structured approach to antenatal breastfeeding education in India.
Breastfeeding promotion in facilities is critical for achieving global health targets, as outlined in WHO implementation guidance.
Studies indicate that antenatal counseling can increase exclusive breastfeeding rates by up to 30%.
reference_quote: Clinical Protocol #19 (2024) emphasizes the importance of breastfeeding promotion during the prenatal period to improve maternal and infant health outcomes.
chunk_heading: BREASTFEEDING & LACTATION MANAGEMENT;ANTENATAL LACTATION RISK ASSESSMENT AND COUNSELING;
subtopic: ANTENATAL LACTATION RISK ASSESSMENT AND COUNSELING
